mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate and pushes investors into risk assets, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as long as policy remains accommodative.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16 percent as of 2012-10, consistent with ZIRP
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly positive (p>0.05) during periods of sustained near-zero rates.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals improving labor market conditions and consumer income prospects, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines more than 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.9 percent in 2012-10, down from earlier 2012 highs
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 20-day forward returns are negative or not significantly different from baseline non-triggered returns (t-test p>0.05) across a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts and retail sales indicates a broad-based consumption and construction upcycle that reinforces corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter when both series rise together but not when only one does.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 872k units in 2012-09 reflecting post-crisis housing recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint HOUST-RSAFS momentum interaction term versus 60-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the interaction effect is no stronger than either single-series effect alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing activity and near-term corporate output, generating a short-lived positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO monthly prints are not significantly greater than returns following negative prints (p>0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero interest rate policy regime, stable low CPI inflation validates continued Fed accommodation and quantitative easing, supporting sustained equity valuation multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship weakens once the regime shifts toward tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.751 in 2012-10 indicating benign inflation
fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16 percent consistent with ZIRP
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy regime with quantitative easing
failure_condition: The positive association between low stable CPI and 60-day forward returns reverses or becomes insignificant (p>0.05) once FEDFUNDS begins a sustained hiking cycle, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 within the ZIRP regime sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive payroll growth and declining unemployment rate jointly confirm a genuine labor market expansion rather than a statistical artifact of falling labor force participation, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month only when both signals co-occur.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-growth and UNRATE-decline interaction fails to produce forward 20d returns significantly higher than instances where only one of the two conditions holds (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in retail sales growth crossing above its recent trailing trend signals stronger-than-expected consumer demand, prompting a short-horizon positive repricing of S&P 500 equities within the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds trailing 6-month average growth by more than 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered RSAFS acceleration events are not significantly greater than unconditional baseline returns (p>0.05) across a 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, confirmed real GDP growth alongside rising industrial production signals durable economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; the effect is muted or reversed outside recovery regimes such as late-cycle expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13616.2 billion chained dollars as of 2012-Q3
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns conditional on positive GDPC1 and INDPRO growth are not significantly different from unconditional baseline returns (p>0.05), or the relationship reverses sign outside the recovery regime sample.
